posts.title: Welcome to ReidCodex
posts.body: ReidCodex is a place to publish notes, guides, and updates. This is a seed post — log in to the admin dashboard to create your own.

posts.title: How to write your first guide
posts.body: Head to /login, sign in with your admin credentials, then use the dashboard to add a new post. Pick a category and it will show up here automatically.

posts.title: Changelog: v1.0
posts.body: First release. Public site with category filtering, plus a password-protected admin dashboard backed by a JSON file.

posts.title: Handy resources
posts.body: A spot to collect links and references you want to keep around. Replace this with your own.

posts.title: Alef — א
posts.body: Alef (א) is the 1st letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Alef. Sound: silent — a glottal stop that carries its vowel. Numeric value (gematria): 1. Pictograph: an ox or bull's head: strength, leadership, primacy, and the oneness of God.

posts.title: Bet — ב
posts.body: Bet (ב) is the 2nd letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Bet (Vet). Sound: "b" (a soft "v" without the dagesh). Numeric value (gematria): 2. Pictograph: a house or tent: household, family, dwelling, and "in/within".

posts.title: Gimel — ג
posts.body: Gimel (ג) is the 3rd letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Gimel. Sound: "g" as in "go". Numeric value (gematria): 3. Pictograph: a camel or foot: to lift up, to walk, movement, pride, and giving (gemilut).

posts.title: Dalet — ד
posts.body: Dalet (ד) is the 4th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Dalet. Sound: "d". Numeric value (gematria): 4. Pictograph: a door: a doorway, pathway, entrance, and humility (dal, the poor).

posts.title: He — ה
posts.body: He (ה) is the 5th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: He (Hei). Sound: "h". Numeric value (gematria): 5. Pictograph: a window, or a person with arms raised: behold, to reveal, breath, expression.

posts.title: Vav — ו
posts.body: Vav (ו) is the 6th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Vav (Waw). Sound: "v"; as a vowel, "o" or "u". Numeric value (gematria): 6. Pictograph: a nail, hook, or peg: to join, to connect, "and," and security.

posts.title: Zayin — ז
posts.body: Zayin (ז) is the 7th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Zayin. Sound: "z". Numeric value (gematria): 7. Pictograph: a weapon or sword: to cut, to nourish, and a crowned implement.

posts.title: Het — ח
posts.body: Het (ח) is the 8th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Chet. Sound: a guttural "ch" (as in Bach). Numeric value (gematria): 8. Pictograph: a fence or wall: to separate, to protect, an inner chamber, and life (chai).

posts.title: Tet — ט
posts.body: Tet (ט) is the 9th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Tet. Sound: an emphatic "t". Numeric value (gematria): 9. Pictograph: a basket or coiled snake: to surround, to contain, to twist, and good (tov).

posts.title: Yod — י
posts.body: Yod (י) is the 10th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Yod. Sound: "y". Numeric value (gematria): 10. Pictograph: an arm and closed hand: a deed, work, to make; the smallest letter — great things begin small.

posts.title: Kaf — כ
posts.body: Kaf (כ) is the 11th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Kaf (Khaf). Sound: "k" (a soft "kh" without the dagesh). Numeric value (gematria): 20. Pictograph: an open or cupped palm: to cover, to open, to bend, and to allow. Its final (sofit) form is ך.

posts.title: Lamed — ל
posts.body: Lamed (ל) is the 12th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Lamed. Sound: "l". Numeric value (gematria): 30. Pictograph: a shepherd's staff or goad: to teach, to learn, to guide; the tallest letter.

posts.title: Mem — מ
posts.body: Mem (מ) is the 13th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Mem. Sound: "m". Numeric value (gematria): 40. Pictograph: water: chaos and depth, the mighty, the masses, and flowing wisdom. Its final (sofit) form is ם.

posts.title: Nun — נ
posts.body: Nun (נ) is the 14th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Nun. Sound: "n". Numeric value (gematria): 50. Pictograph: a fish or sprouting seed: life, activity, continuity, an heir, and faithfulness. Its final (sofit) form is ן.

posts.title: Samekh — ס
posts.body: Samekh (ס) is the 15th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Samekh. Sound: "s". Numeric value (gematria): 60. Pictograph: a prop or support (a thorn/twist): to support, to uphold, to encircle and protect.

posts.title: Ayin — ע
posts.body: Ayin (ע) is the 16th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Ayin. Sound: a silent guttural ('a). Numeric value (gematria): 70. Pictograph: an eye: to see, to perceive, to know, insight and experience.

posts.title: Pe — פ
posts.body: Pe (פ) is the 17th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Pe (Fe). Sound: "p" (a soft "f"/"ph" without the dagesh). Numeric value (gematria): 80. Pictograph: a mouth: to speak, a word, to open, an edge. Its final (sofit) form is ף.

posts.title: Tsadi — צ
posts.body: Tsadi (צ) is the 18th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Tsadi. Sound: "ts" / "tz". Numeric value (gematria): 90. Pictograph: a fish-hook or a person bowing: to catch, to desire, need, and righteousness (tzaddik). Its final (sofit) form is ץ.

posts.title: Qof — ק
posts.body: Qof (ק) is the 19th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Qof. Sound: a deep, uvular "q". Numeric value (gematria): 100. Pictograph: the back of the head, or the sun on the horizon: behind, the last/least, time and cycles, to encircle.

posts.title: Resh — ר
posts.body: Resh (ר) is the 20th letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Resh. Sound: "r". Numeric value (gematria): 200. Pictograph: the head of a person: first, top, beginning, chief.

posts.title: Shin — ש
posts.body: Shin (ש) is the 21st letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Shin (Sin). Sound: "sh" (or "s" as sin). Numeric value (gematria): 300. Pictograph: two or three front teeth: to consume, to press, sharpness; a symbol of Shaddai.

posts.title: Tav — ת
posts.body: Tav (ת) is the 22nd letter of the Hebrew alphabet. Transliteration: Tav. Sound: "t". Numeric value (gematria): 400. Pictograph: a mark, sign, or cross: a sign, a covenant, to seal, and completion — the end of the alphabet.
